notes: PROPOSED labels, pending human ratification (agent-proposed 2026-07-12; the corpus is CODEOWNERS-gated, versioned data). Ground truth established by source-inspection of @supabase/mcp-server-supabase at github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp, tag mcp-server-supabase-v0.8.2 (packages/mcp-server-supabase/src/tools), cross-checked against a live stdio tools/list capture of the published npm package on 2026-07-11. Every tool carries full, honest MCP annotations, so all 29 rows are consistent and the server is a pure precision guard. It exists in the corpus for one row in particular: generate_typescript_types returns generated TypeScript source as reply content while correctly declaring readOnlyHint:true — the reply-content-vs-world-state case that made write-family verb lexemes for generate/install mis-read read-only tools as writes. A linter that flags any tool on this server is wrong.

provenance.notes: Read over vendor documentation content; the honest read-only declaration is consistent.

provenance.notes: The reverse of pause_project; a reversible state flip honestly declared non-destructive.

provenance.source: github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp, packages/mcp-server-supabase/src/tools/development-tools.ts @ mcp-server-supabase-v0.8.2 — generate_typescript_types returns development.generateTypescriptTypes(project_id) — generated TypeScript source returned as reply content; the handler writes no state
provenance.notes: The reply-content regression row: the verb generate names a tool that produces content in the reply, not state in the world. Its readOnlyHint:true is correct, so the honest verdict is consistent — a write-as-readonly flag here is a linter false positive (the vocabulary screened generate out of the write families for exactly this reason).
